other_observers: Ted Floyd and Tony Leukering also have seen the specimen with me; Found by Alan Carpenter. Specimen brought to my attention by Ted Floyd. I delivered the specimen to Tony Leukering.
specific_location: Found near the intersection of Evergreen and 19th street in Boulder
description: Small yellow-throated, bluish and green backed warbler with white wing bars. Maxilla dark gray, mandible nearly white. Face bluish-gray, no eye-arcs visible (may be sunken into eye sockets.) Auriculars bluish-gray. Crown feathers bluish-gray, tipped with green. Saddle greenish yellow. Chest only slightly more orange-yellow than rest of throat and breast. Underparts below breast unmarked white. Wings dark gray, flight feathers with thin pale edging. Tail a bit lighter gray than the wings. Feet and legs almost black.Behaviors: found dead on sidewalkCall: not heardPlumage: female or immature male
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
